Apple___Apple_scab.name: Apple Scab
Apple___Apple_scab.cure: Apply fungicides containing captan or myclobutanil. Remove and destroy infected leaves and fruit.
Apple___Apple_scab.prevention: Plant resistant varieties. Prune for air circulation. Rake and remove fallen leaves in autumn.
Apple___Apple_scab.description: Apple scab causes olive-green to dark brown velvety spots on leaves and corky lesions on fruit.

Apple___Black_rot.name: Black Rot
Apple___Black_rot.cure: Apply captan-based fungicides. Remove mummified fruit and cankers; burn or bury debris.
Apple___Black_rot.prevention: Prune annually for airflow. Sanitize tools. Remove overwintering mummies from trees and ground.
Apple___Black_rot.description: Black rot causes frogeye leaf spots, fruit rot, and branch cankers on apple trees.

Apple___Cedar_apple_rust.name: Cedar Apple Rust
Apple___Cedar_apple_rust.cure: Apply myclobutanil or propiconazole during bloom and early fruit set as labeled.
Apple___Cedar_apple_rust.prevention: Remove nearby juniper/cedar hosts if possible. Use resistant apple cultivars.
Apple___Cedar_apple_rust.description: Bright yellow-orange leaf spots; gelatinous horn-like structures may appear on cedar hosts.

Apple___healthy.name: Healthy Apple
Apple___healthy.prevention: Maintain balanced pruning, drip irrigation, and seasonal dormant sprays per local extension advice.
Apple___healthy.description: Apple foliage appears healthy with no significant disease symptoms.

Blueberry___healthy.name: Healthy Blueberry
Blueberry___healthy.prevention: Use acidic soil (pH 4.5–5.5), mulch with pine bark, and ensure consistent moisture.
Blueberry___healthy.description: Blueberry leaves look healthy without notable disease signs.

Cherry___healthy.name: Healthy Cherry
Cherry___healthy.prevention: Prune for airflow, monitor for borers, and avoid overhead irrigation on foliage.

Cherry___Powdery_mildew.name: Powdery Mildew (Cherry)
Cherry___Powdery_mildew.cure: Apply sulfur, potassium bicarbonate, or myclobutanil. Remove severely infected shoots.
Cherry___Powdery_mildew.prevention: Plant resistant varieties. Avoid dense planting and shaded damp conditions.
Cherry___Powdery_mildew.description: White powdery fungal growth on leaves and shoots of cherry.

Corn___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray_leaf_sp.name: Cercospora / Gray Leaf Spot
Corn___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray_leaf_sp.cure: Apply strobilurin or triazole fungicides if economically justified. Rotate to non-host crops.
Corn___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray_leaf_sp.prevention: Use tolerant hybrids, till residue, and rotate crops for 1–2 years.
Corn___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray_leaf_sp.description: Rectangular gray-tan lesions on corn leaves, often reducing photosynthetic area.

Corn___Common_rust_.name: Common Rust (Corn)
Corn___Common_rust_.cure: Fungicide application (e.g., propiconazole) if infection is early and yield risk is high.
Corn___Common_rust_.prevention: Plant rust-resistant hybrids. Scout fields regularly during humid weather.
Corn___Common_rust_.description: Reddish-brown pustules on upper and lower corn leaf surfaces.

Corn___Northern_Leaf_Blight.name: Northern Leaf Blight
Corn___Northern_Leaf_Blight.cure: Apply fungicides containing chlorothalonil or mancozeb at early lesion stages.
Corn___Northern_Leaf_Blight.prevention: Use resistant hybrids, rotate crops, and bury infected residue.
Corn___Northern_Leaf_Blight.description: Long elliptical gray-green lesions that turn tan on corn leaves.

Corn___healthy.name: Healthy Corn
Corn___healthy.prevention: Balanced NPK fertilization, timely weed control, and adequate spacing for airflow.
Corn___healthy.description: Corn leaves appear healthy without significant foliar disease.

Grape___Black_rot.name: Black Rot (Grape)
Grape___Black_rot.cure: Apply mancozeb or captan on a protective schedule. Remove infected clusters and leaves.
Grape___Black_rot.prevention: Prune for sun and air exposure. Remove mummified berries. Keep canopy open.
Grape___Black_rot.description: Circular brown leaf spots and black shriveled fruit (mummies) on grapevines.

Grape___Esca(Black_Measles).name: Esca (Black Measles)
Grape___Esca(Black_Measles).cure: No cure for infected wood. Remove severely affected vines. Trunk surgery only by trained growers.
Grape___Esca(Black_Measles).prevention: Use certified planting material. Avoid large pruning wounds; protect cuts.
Grape___Esca(Black_Measles).description: Chronic vine disease with leaf tiger-stripe patterns, dieback, and berry spotting.

Grape___Leaf_blight(Isariopsis_Leaf_Spot.name: Isariopsis Leaf Spot
Grape___Leaf_blight(Isariopsis_Leaf_Spot.cure: Apply copper or mancozeb sprays. Remove heavily infected leaves during dormant pruning.
Grape___Leaf_blight(Isariopsis_Leaf_Spot.prevention: Improve canopy airflow. Remove debris. Avoid prolonged leaf wetness.
Grape___Leaf_blight(Isariopsis_Leaf_Spot.description: Brown irregular leaf spots that may coalesce, causing early defoliation on grape.

Grape___healthy.name: Healthy Grape
Grape___healthy.prevention: Monitor regularly, maintain open canopy, and apply preventive fungicides per season.

Orange___Haunglongbing(Citrus_greening).name: Citrus Greening (HLB)
Orange___Haunglongbing(Citrus_greening).cure: No cure. Remove severely infected trees to protect the orchard. Control Asian citrus psyllid.
Orange___Haunglongbing(Citrus_greening).prevention: Use certified disease-free nursery stock. Scout and spray for psyllids per local program.
Orange___Haunglongbing(Citrus_greening).description: Blotchy mottle on leaves, yellow shoots, misshapen bitter fruit; spread by psyllid vector.

Peach___Bacterial_spot.name: Bacterial Spot (Peach)
Peach___Bacterial_spot.cure: Apply copper sprays during dormancy and low-rate copper + antibiotics where legal and labeled.
Peach___Bacterial_spot.prevention: Plant resistant cultivars. Avoid overhead irrigation. Maintain tree vigor.
Peach___Bacterial_spot.description: Small dark leaf spots, shot-hole effect, and fruit lesions on peach and nectarine.

Peach___healthy.name: Healthy Peach
Peach___healthy.prevention: Annual pruning, brown rot prevention program, and balanced fertilization.

Pepper,bell___Bacterial_spot.name: Bacterial Spot (Bell Pepper)
Pepper,bell___Bacterial_spot.cure: Apply copper-based bactericides. Remove infected transplants and debris.
Pepper,bell___Bacterial_spot.prevention: Use certified seed. Rotate solanaceous crops. Avoid working wet plants.
Pepper,bell___Bacterial_spot.description: Water-soaked leaf spots turning brown; fruit may show raised scabby lesions.

Pepper,bell___healthy.name: Healthy Bell Pepper
Pepper,bell___healthy.prevention: Drip irrigation, mulch, and crop rotation away from tomato/potato.

Potato___Early_blight.name: Early Blight (Potato)
Potato___Early_blight.cure: Apply chlorothalonil or mancozeb. Remove lower infected foliage.
Potato___Early_blight.prevention: Rotate crops. Use certified seed tubers. Maintain adequate potassium nutrition.
Potato___Early_blight.description: Dark brown concentric-ring spots on lower leaves; yellowing and defoliation follow.

Potato___Late_blight.name: Late Blight (Potato)
Potato___Late_blight.cure: Apply fungicides immediately (chlorothalonil, cymoxanil, or mandipropamid per label). Destroy infected plants.
Potato___Late_blight.prevention: Plant certified seed. Scout during cool wet weather. Avoid overhead irrigation.
Potato___Late_blight.description: Water-soaked lesions spreading rapidly; white sporulation under humid conditions.

Potato___healthy.name: Healthy Potato
Potato___healthy.prevention: Hill soil around stems, rotate crops, and monitor for early blight/late blight.

Raspberry___healthy.name: Healthy Raspberry
Raspberry___healthy.prevention: Prune canes, ensure trellising and airflow, and remove wild brambles nearby.

Soybean___healthy.name: Healthy Soybean
Soybean___healthy.prevention: Use inoculated seed, scout for pests, and maintain crop rotation.

Squash___Powdery_mildew.name: Powdery Mildew (Squash)
Squash___Powdery_mildew.cure: Apply sulfur, potassium bicarbonate, or horticultural oils. Remove heavily infected leaves.
Squash___Powdery_mildew.prevention: Plant resistant varieties. Space plants widely. Avoid excess nitrogen.
Squash___Powdery_mildew.description: White powdery patches on squash and pumpkin leaves, often in mid to late season.

Strawberry___Leaf_scorch.name: Leaf Scorch (Strawberry)
Strawberry___Leaf_scorch.cure: Apply captan or thiophanate-methyl as labeled. Remove infected leaves after harvest.
Strawberry___Leaf_scorch.prevention: Use resistant varieties. Ensure drainage and avoid leaf wetness overnight.
Strawberry___Leaf_scorch.description: Purple spots on leaves that turn brown; may cause scorched appearance.

Strawberry___healthy.name: Healthy Strawberry
Strawberry___healthy.prevention: Renovate beds after harvest, mulch, and rotate planting sites every few years.

Tomato___Bacterial_spot.name: Bacterial Spot (Tomato)
Tomato___Bacterial_spot.cure: Copper sprays plus mancozeb (where labeled). Remove infected seedlings.
Tomato___Bacterial_spot.prevention: Use pathogen-free seed. Rotate crops. Avoid overhead watering.
Tomato___Bacterial_spot.description: Small dark greasy spots on leaves; fruit may show raised scabs.

Tomato___Early_blight.name: Early Blight (Tomato)
Tomato___Early_blight.cure: Apply chlorothalonil or mancozeb. Prune lower leaves and stake plants.
Tomato___Early_blight.prevention: Rotate crops. Mulch soil. Use drip irrigation.
Tomato___Early_blight.description: Brown lesions with concentric rings on lower tomato leaves.

Tomato___Late_blight.name: Late Blight (Tomato)
Tomato___Late_blight.cure: Apply fungicides immediately; remove and bag infected plants. Do not compost.
Tomato___Late_blight.prevention: Plant resistant varieties. Scout during cool wet periods. Improve airflow.
Tomato___Late_blight.description: Large irregular water-soaked lesions on leaves and dark firm spots on fruit.

Tomato___Leaf_Mold.name: Leaf Mold (Tomato)
Tomato___Leaf_Mold.cure: Improve ventilation in greenhouses. Apply chlorothalonil or labeled fungicides.
Tomato___Leaf_Mold.prevention: Lower humidity, prune suckers, and use resistant cultivars indoors.
Tomato___Leaf_Mold.description: Yellow patches on upper leaf surface with olive-green mold underneath.

Tomato___Septoria_leaf_spot.name: Septoria Leaf Spot
Tomato___Septoria_leaf_spot.cure: Remove infected lower leaves. Apply chlorothalonil or copper fungicides.
Tomato___Septoria_leaf_spot.prevention: Rotate crops. Mulch. Avoid working wet foliage.
Tomato___Septoria_leaf_spot.description: Small circular spots with dark borders and gray centers on tomato leaves.

Tomato___Spider_mites Two-spotted_spider.name: Two-Spotted Spider Mite
Tomato___Spider_mites Two-spotted_spider.cure: Apply insecticidal soap or miticides (e.g., abamectin where labeled). Increase humidity for greenhouse crops.
Tomato___Spider_mites Two-spotted_spider.prevention: Avoid drought stress. Monitor undersides of leaves. Encourage predatory mites.
Tomato___Spider_mites Two-spotted_spider.description: Fine stippling on leaves, bronzing, and fine webbing under severe infestation.

Tomato___Target_Spot.name: Target Spot (Tomato)
Tomato___Target_Spot.cure: Apply azoxystrobin or chlorothalonil. Remove infected debris.
Tomato___Target_Spot.prevention: Rotate crops. Improve spacing and airflow. Avoid overhead irrigation.
Tomato___Target_Spot.description: Brown lesions with concentric rings resembling a target on tomato leaves.

Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.name: Tomato Yellow Leaf Curl Virus
Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.cure: No cure. Remove infected plants. Control whiteflies with labeled insecticides.
Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.prevention: Use TYLCV-resistant varieties. Use insect-proof nets in nurseries.
Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.description: Upward leaf curling, yellowing, and stunted growth spread by whiteflies.

Tomato___Tomato_mosaic_virus.name: Tomato Mosaic Virus
Tomato___Tomato_mosaic_virus.cure: No cure. Remove infected plants. Disinfect tools with 10% bleach solution.
Tomato___Tomato_mosaic_virus.prevention: Use certified seed. Do not smoke near plants (TMV). Control aphids.
Tomato___Tomato_mosaic_virus.description: Mottled light and dark green leaf pattern; distorted leaves and reduced yield.

Tomato___healthy.name: Healthy Tomato
Tomato___healthy.prevention: Stake plants, mulch, balanced feeding, and regular scouting for pests and disease.
Tomato___healthy.description: Tomato plants appear healthy with no significant disease symptoms.
